description: A solid whose constituent particles repeat in an ordered structure is 'kirae', especially when that structure forms a visible bounded body. A crystal may be cloudy, coloured, or too small to see without equipment. 'phielo' (glass) lacks the same long-range order; a mineral name can identify the substance.
articulatory_notes: Unvoiced 'k' forms where the back of the tongue meets the soft palate, then releases into high-front 'i'. A tap or brief trill opens onto stressed 'a', followed by a clean hiatus into final front 'e'.
usage_notes: The object classifier 'themo' is available for a single crystal; a cluster or quantity needs none. A mineral name identifies the substance, and a scientific source can add its lattice classification.

semantic_domains.nature: ordered mineral or other solid structures found in natural settings
semantic_domains.physical: a solid with a repeating internal arrangement
